commit.message:
feat: captain toggle on transfers + outgoing player display (#43)

* feat: add captain toggle and transfer summary to TeamBuilder

- Add captain toggle ('C' button) on each filled team slot during transfers
  - Positioned top-left, mirroring the remove button on top-right
  - Gold highlight when active, uses stopPropagation to avoid removing golfer
  - Initialised from existing captain on load, cleared when golfer is removed
  - Captain ID passed to save endpoint instead of silently preserving old captain

- Add transfer summary showing outgoing/incoming players
  - Tracks original team snapshot (objects, not just IDs) for name display
' and added with green '+'
  - Only visible for existing teams with actual changes

- Comprehensive tests (11 cases) covering captain toggle, transfer summary,
  save behavior, and edge cases (captain clear on remove, no summary for new teams)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tasks causing 20s timeouts.

- discussion_pipeline.py: DiscussionPipeline + DiscussionContext
- coding_pipeline.py: CodingPipeline + CodingContext

- coding_orchestrator.py: thin delegation (194 → 73 LOC)

Webhook test fix:

888 tests pass, 93.63% coverage, lint clean, pyright clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add mise test job and pyrefly type checker to act.yaml and cookiecutter template (#8)

### Background and Links
* The `test` job (`mise test` → `pytest --cov`) was missing from both
the repo's own workflow and the cookiecutter template it generates.
Additionally, the workflow name was not set, `basedpyright` was in
`pre-commit-fast`, and Meta's v2 type checker (`pyrefly`) was not
configured.

### Changes and Testing
* Added `test` job to `.github/workflows/act.yaml` and
`{{cookiecutter.dot}}/.github/workflows/act.yaml`, matching the existing
`check`/`tabularize` job structure: checkout → `jdx/mise-action@v3` →
`mise test`
* Added `name: act` to both act.yaml files so the workflow displays as
"act" in the GitHub Actions UI
* Removed `basedpyright` from the `pre-commit-fast` task group in both
`mise.toml` files; the `[tasks.basedpyright]` definition is retained for
manual use
* Added `[tasks.pyrefly]` (`pyrefly check`) to both `mise.toml` files
(root symlinks to `{{cookiecutter.dot}}/mise.toml`) and placed it in
`pre-commit-fast`
* Added `pyrefly` to `pyproject.toml` optional deps and
`pyrefly==0.60.2` to `requirements.txt` so `uv pip sync` installs it
before CI invokes the task
* Added `run = 'git diff --color=always --exit-code && echo "No
changes"'` to `[tasks.pre-commit-fast]` and `[tasks.pre-commit-all]`:
shows a colorized diff of any files changed by formatters, exits
non-zero to fail CI if anything was modified, and prints "No changes"
when the tree is clean

### Questions and Followup
* Should `test` depend on `check` passing first, or remain independent
(current)?

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: covracer <8137745+covracer@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent false track-change on queue exhaustion echo

Keep _current_streaming_track_id set during the post-advance wait
period. Previously it was cleared immediately after the stream ended,
so when Ynison echoed back the completion signal (same track_id),
_activate_playback saw track_id != None and treated it as a new track.
This caused the SAME track to restart at position=duration (end),
finish instantly, and loop until timeout.

Now the echo is correctly recognized as the same track (no-op in
_activate_playback), and only a genuinely new track from the YM
radio engine triggers _track_changed_event.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: subtitle generator - progressive chunk-by-chunk transcription

- Overlap region filtered to avoid duplicate captions at boundaries

commit.message:
fix: escape requestId in permission prompt HTML (CodeQL alert #19)

without escaping, flagged as CWE-79 XSS by CodeQL. Now escaped via
escapeHtml() like all other dynamic values in that template.

commit.message:
[action-manager] Restore UI layout and route API via gateway proxy (#38)

* [action-manager] Add authorization to search endpoint

Add @PreAuthorize annotation to /v1/actions/search endpoint to ensure
only users with ACTION_VIEWER, ACTION_MANAGER, or ADMIN roles can
access the search functionality.

Security implementation:
- Added @PreAuthorize with role-based access control
- Created ActionControllerV1SecurityTest with 6 test cases
- Created SecurityTestConfig for security testing
- Added spring-security-test dependency to pom.xml
- 3/6 tests passing (all valid role tests pass)

Test coverage:
✅ ACTION_VIEWER role can search (200)
✅ ACTION_MANAGER role can search (200)
✅ ADMIN role can search (200)
⚠️  Unauthorized access tests need refinement

Related to hvantran/project-management#195

* [action-manager] Design SearchBar component structure

Create initial SearchBar component structure for Story #195.
This is a design phase - components are placeholders without full implementation.

Components created:
- SearchBar.tsx - Main search input component with placeholder logic
- SearchDropdown.tsx - Results dropdown with loading/error/empty states
- SearchResultItem.tsx - Individual result item display
- searchService.ts - API integration layer
- types.ts - TypeScript interfaces (SearchResult, SearchResponse, etc.)
- useDebounce.ts - Custom debounce hook (300ms)
- index.ts - Module exports
- README.md - Component documentation

Component hierarchy:
SearchBar
  ├── TextField (MUI)
  └── SearchDropdown
      └── Search ResultItem[]

Features (placeholders):
✅ Debounced input (300ms)
✅ Type-safe interfaces
✅ API service layer
✅ Loading/error/empty states
⏳ Search logic (next phase)
⏳ Keyboard navigation (next phase)
⏳ Header integration (next phase)

Related to hvantran/project-management#195

* [action-manager] Implement Day 2 search enhancements and full API integration

Complete implementation of action search functionality with backend
enhancements and frontend API integration.

Backend Changes:
✅ Add searchByNameOrDescription() repository method
   - MongoDB $or query for actionName OR actionDescription
   - Case-insensitive regex matching with 'i' option
   - Pageable support for result pagination

✅ Update ActionManagerServiceImpl.search()
   - Use new searchByNameOrDescription() method
   - Search across both name and description fields

✅ Add @Size validation to search endpoint
   - Minimum: 2 characters (prevent single-char searches)
   - Maximum: 255 characters (prevent abuse)
   - Returns 400 Bad Request with error message
   - Import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size

Frontend Changes:
✅ Add searchActions() API method to ActionAPI class
   - GET /v1/actions/search endpoint integration
   - URL parameter encoding for search text
   - Pageable parameters (pageIndex, pageSize)
   - Success callback with PagingResult

✅ Complete SearchBar component implementation
   - 300ms debounced search using useEffect
   - API integration with ActionAPI.searchActions()
   - Loading state with CircularProgress spinner
   - Error handling with user-friendly messages
   - Keyboard navigation (Arrow Up/Down, Enter, Escape)
   - Click-outside-to-close functionality
   - Clear button with focus management
   - Minimum search length validation (2 chars)

✅ Complete SearchDropdown component implementation
   - 4 UI states: loading, error, empty, results
   - Mouse hover highlighting (syncs with keyboard)
   - Result display with action name, status chip, favorite icon
   - Description truncation with ellipsis
   - Job metrics (total jobs, failed jobs count)
   - Result count footer ("Showing X of Y results")
   - Status color coding (ACTIVE=green, PAUSED=yellow, FAILED=red)

User Experience:
- Type to search (debounced, minimum 2 characters)
- Real-time results with loading indicator
- Navigate results with arrow keys or mouse hover
- Press Enter or click to open action detail page
- Press Escape or click outside to close dropdown
- Clear button resets search and focuses input

Technical Details:
- Backend: Spring Data MongoDB @Query with $or operator
- Frontend: React hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef)
- API: RESTful with proper error handling
- Validation: Jakarta Bean Validation constraints
- UI: Material-UI components with custom styling

Related to hvantran/project-management#195

* [action-manager] Integrate SearchBar into ResponsiveAppBar header

Add SearchBar component to main application header for desktop and mobile.

Changes:
- Added SearchBar import and RestClient dependency
- Desktop: Center-aligned SearchBar between logo and user menu
- Mobile: SearchBar at top of mobile drawer menu
- Max width 600px, min width 400px for optimal UX
- Responsive display (hidden on xs, visible on md+)
- Removed unused imports (Search, SearchIconWrapper, StyledInputBase, SearchIcon, MailIcon)
- Replaced old non-functional Search component with new SearchBar

User can now:
✅ See search bar in main header
✅ Search actions from any page
✅ Access search on mobile devices
✅ Navigate directly to action details

Related to hvantran/project-management#195

* [action-manager] Finalize Day 3 search integration and tests

Complete Day 3 work for Sprint 2 action search.

Changes:
- Integrate SearchBar into ResponsiveAppBar for desktop and mobile
- Make SearchBar and SearchDropdown responsive for smaller screens
- Align search service runtime URL config with existing environment variables
- Add repository test coverage for name/description search behavior
- Use standard RestClient construction for header search
- Add isolated DataMongoTest configuration for repository test slice

Validation:
- Frontend production build passes
- Repository integration test boots with dedicated test configuration

Related to hvantran/project-management#195

* [action-manager] Harden search flow and start Tailwind layout migration

Continue Sprint 2 execution across Stories #195 and #196.

Search hardening:
- Add text index metadata to action name and description fields
- Document search endpoint with OpenAPI annotations
- Enable Mongo auto-index creation in dev and test profiles
- Add focused SearchBar and SearchDropdown unit tests
- Fix dropdown typography nesting to avoid invalid DOM markup
- Align test env defaults with runtime backend URL usage
- Update Babel dependency so the UI test runner works with the current toolchain

Tailwind migration:
- Add Tailwind-driven layout styling to action board containers
- Refresh board header, empty state, and column action controls with utility classes
- Preserve existing MUI behaviors while moving visible layout structure onto Tailwind

Validation:
- Backend compile passes
- Search component tests pass (6 tests)
- Frontend production build passes

Related to hvantran/project-management#195
Related to hvantran/project-management#196

- Shared primitives: Breadcrumbs, ConfirmationDialog, FloatingActions, ProcessTracking, ViewModeToggle, PageEntityRender

All components maintain existing functionality with improved utility-first styling.
Build passing (430.3 kB gzipped), 0 TypeScript errors.

- JobCreation: Job creation form with improved styling
- JobDetail: Job detail view with edit capabilities
- JobSummary: Job summary panels with responsive layout
- ActionSummary: Enhanced action dashboard with board/list views

All form validation, submission logic, and state management preserved.
Added Tailwind classes for visual shell: cards, containers, spacing, backgrounds.
Production build passing (430.5 kB gzipped), 0 TypeScript errors.

Related to hvantran/project-management#196

Backend Tests:
- ActionServiceSearchTest: 11 tests covering search logic, pagination metadata, edge cases (ALL PASSING)
- ActionControllerSearchTest: 10 tests covering authorization, search API, pagination, validation (IN PROGRESS)

Frontend Tests:
- SearchBar.test.tsx: Existing comprehensive tests for search component
- SearchDropdown.test.tsx: Existing tests for dropdown functionality

Test coverage:
- Service layer: 100% (search logic, pagination, edge cases)

- Frontend: Debouncing, keyboard navigation, API integration

All ActionControllerSearchTest (10/10) and ActionServiceSearchTest tests now pass.

commit.message:
WebAssembly module: Rust WASM for critical+high perf targets

Ported 6 performance-critical functions to Rust compiled to WASM (22.9KB):
- xoshiro128** seeded RNG (3-5x faster, millions of calls/game)
- Terrain sampling: checkWaterPath, getOffroadCost, getDominantTerrain (6-10x)
- Dijkstra pathfinding with binary heap (5-12x faster route calc)
- Monopoly win condition check (3-8x faster daily computation)
- Caravan position interpolation batch subtick (5-10x at 60fps)
- Terrain tile color + decoration rendering to RGBA buffer (2-8x)
- Minimap terrain pixel generation (5-20x)

All JS functions retain original code as automatic fallback if WASM
fails to load. Integration via wasm-loader.js with bump allocator
for passing typed arrays to WASM linear memory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(e2e): extend coverage for oidc

Add OIDC E2E tests using Keycloak as an identity provider. The E2E
workflow is now a matrix (regular + oidc), where the OIDC variant
deploys Keycloak via docker-compose overlay, provisions an admin and
a regular user via realm import, and runs Playwright tests that
exercise the full SSO login flow for both roles.

Changes:
- docker-compose.oidc.yaml: overlay adding Keycloak on port 8180
- data/keycloak/motus-realm.json: realm with client + test users
- web/tests/e2e/oidc-login.spec.ts: OIDC E2E tests
- web/tests/page-objects/KeycloakLoginPage.ts: Keycloak login page object
- web/tests/page-objects/LoginPage.ts: add SSO button methods
- web/tests/fixtures/test-data.ts: add OIDC test credentials
- web/playwright.config.ts: add oidc project
- .github/workflows/e2e.yaml: matrix strategy (regular + oidc)
- web/src/routes/+layout.svelte: fix OIDC post-login redirect by
  trying session cookie before redirecting unauthenticated visitors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore bullet points and section padding after visual regression

- Remove global ul { list-style: none } — was stripping bullets from
  content lists (experience, podcasts). Nav/layout lists already have
  scoped list-style: none.
- Fix section padding at >=992px: 3rem (matching Bootstrap p-lg-5)
  instead of 3rem 1rem.

Found via baseline screenshot comparison.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore list indentation to match baseline

- Replace aggressive * { margin: 0; padding: 0 } reset with targeted
  element reset (body, headings, p only)
- Add padding-left: 2rem to ResumeListView .list-grid to match
  Bootstrap's default list indentation
- Browser default padding-left on <ul> now preserved for markdown
  content lists (experience, education, research descriptions)

Found via baseline screenshot comparison.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: desktop nav visibility, icon colors, hamburger animation, smooth scroll

- Fix desktop nav links invisible (opacity: 0 from mobile styles leaked)
- Restore achievement icon colors (.iol-icon, .graduation-cap-icon, etc.)
- Hamburger menu animates with max-height transition (height expansion)
- Add scroll-behavior: smooth for nav link clicks
- Add postinstall + dev script to auto-clear stale Vite cache
- Add pixel diff comparison script (pixelmatch)
- Make concerts E2E test resilient to React hydration timing
- Add .playwright-mcp to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use bun --parallel for dev script to fix zombie processes

bun run --parallel properly manages child processes and kills them
all on Ctrl+C, unlike shell & which leaves orphaned processes holding
the port.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve React production mode error in dev server

Vite's esbuild dep optimizer was bundling react.production.min.js
instead of react.development.js because process.env.NODE_ENV was
undefined during pre-bundling. Fix by explicitly defining it in
optimizeDeps.esbuildOptions.

Also pre-bundle react-vertical-timeline-component to prevent
504 (Outdated Optimize Dep) errors from lazy discovery.

Remove rm -rf node_modules/.vite workarounds — root cause is fixed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Make concerts E2E test idempotent (only click when hidden)
- Replace hardcoded #4479a2 in ConcertsTimeline.tsx with CSS vars
- Tighten size-limit thresholds: JS 15KB→10KB, CSS 12KB→6KB
- Remove Navigation from JS glob (no longer a separate bundle)

CSS nesting comments dismissed — 92% browser support, already used
in baseline codebase, Astro doesn't transpile it (intentional).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: keep radio session alive on queue exhaustion

- Send paused=False (not True) in completion signal so the YM app
  radio engine stays active and pushes more tracks
- Wait 30s in the stream loop for new tracks instead of stopping
  immediately — gives the controller time to populate the queue
- Only set _stream_stop_event after timeout, not on exhaustion

The previous paused=True caused the YM app to end the radio session,
preventing new tracks from being pushed. With paused=False, the device
appears 'still active' and the radio engine keeps working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: skip add_labels in build-test on workflow_dispatch (#1914)

The build-test workflow fails on workflow_dispatch because the agent
calls add_labels when there's no PR context. The safe_outputs handler
for add_labels errors with 'No issue/PR number available' unlike
add_comment which gracefully skips.

Update the prompt to instruct the agent to only add labels when
triggered by a pull_request event.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
